title: Watch: Oil India discovers natural gas deposit in Andaman basin; Puri hails find
content: Oil India Limited has discovered natural gas in the Andaman Sea at the "Sri Vijayapuram-3" exploratory well. This significant find, located 15 km off the Andaman Islands, was confirmed through continuous flaring during initial production testing. The discovery is part of the government's "Samudra Manthan Mission" to boost offshore exploration.

title: Nepal batter achieves rare T20I milestone, joins Samson, Tilak in elite list
content: Nepal's Kushal Bhurtel is on a record-breaking spree at the Asian Games qualifiers, smashing back-to-back T20I centuries. Days after hitting six sixes in an over, he delivered another stunning century, powering Nepal to a massive win. This remarkable feat places him in an elite group of cricketers with consecutive T20I hundreds.

title: At 7.7%, India's GDP growth in FY26 beats slowdown predictions; but will the momentum continue?
content: What does the GDP data tell us about India’s economic prospects? Will the real hit from the US-Iran conflict reflect in the first quarter GDP numbers? And, is India’s long-term growth story under threat? Let’s take a look:

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
content: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: ‘Very big privilege’: Gill opens up on captaining in front of home crowd
content: Captain Shubman Gill emphasizes Test cricket's unparalleled satisfaction as India prepares for their one-off Test against Afghanistan.  Acknowledging the challenging transition from IPL, Gill expressed confidence in young talents and highlighted the significance of this match for building momentum ahead of crucial World Test Championship fixtures. He stressed Afghanistan's formidable progress, urging against underestimation.

title: Unicorn in the USA: Indians aren't stealing American jobs, they're building entire HR depts
content: Immigrants are driving America's innovation, founding 59% of billion-dollar startups, known as unicorns. People of Indian origin lead this trend, establishing 96 such companies. These ventures employ thousands and contribute trillions to the U.S. economy, challenging narratives of immigrants harming the job market. International students also play a crucial role in this entrepreneurial surge.

title: Microsoft HR head Amy Coleman to employees: I want to be transparent about how things are feeling
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman shared employee survey results, highlighting increased employee "energized" and "empowered" feelings, alongside strengths in security and inclusion. However, challenges remain in opportunities for experience broadening, productivity support, and clarity on work's organizational connection. Coleman pledged greater transparency and communication amidst intense change.

title: ENG vs NZ: Gay scripts history, becomes first-ever batter since Strauss to...
content: England opener Emilio Gay marked his Test debut against New Zealand at Lord's with a composed 57 in the second innings. His crucial half-century anchored the hosts against a disciplined pace attack. With this knock, Gay became the first England men's opener to score a debut fifty on home soil since Andrew Strauss in 2004, stabilising the top order.

title: After Annamalai's exit, TN BJP vice president Karu Nagarajan, 15 others resign from party
content: Following K. Annamalai's resignation from the BJP and announcement of a new political movement, significant fallout has hit the Tamil Nadu unit. State vice president Karu Nagarajan and 15 other officials, including state secretary Sumathi Venkatesh, have also quit. Annamalai cited differing visions and a desire for a broader platform, while the state BJP president downplayed the impact.

title: Dario Amodei speaks on leaving Sam Altman's OpenAI to start Anthropic
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has opened up about why he left OpenAI to co-found Anthropic—and it comes down to two things: scaling laws and safety. Speaking on Nikhil Kamath's podcast WTF Is, Amodei said he saw the potential of scaling AI models as early as 2019 with GPT-2, while most colleagues remained skeptical. He also said he wasn't convinced OpenAI had a genuine commitment to building AI responsibly—so he left to do it his way.

title: Nasa reverses evacuation alert order for astronauts aboard ISS
content: Astronauts on the International Space Station were instructed to take shelter in their docked spacecraft and prepare for a potential evacuation Friday. This precautionary measure was enacted after an air leak in the Russian segment showed signs of worsening, prompting specialists to assess and monitor containment efforts.

title: NFL trade rumour: Commanders, Colts eye $4M Patriots star
content: The New England Patriots’ wide receiver Kayshon Boutte is now seeking a trade after the team traded for A.J. Brown and signed Romeo Doubs sparking chaos among fans. While many rival teams have been linked to Kayshon Boutte, it seems the Washington Commanders and the Indianapolis Colts have emerged as potential landing spots for the star player.

title: Morgan Riddle sparks backlash over response to dating article
content: Morgan Riddle recently criticized a New York Times opinion article, calling its message about women “objectively horrific.” The tennis influencer shared her views through Instagram posts and revisited her earlier comments about gender issues. While the debate gained attention online, Taylor Fritz is now focused on returning to action during the upcoming grass-court season in Stuttgart.

title: Travis Kelce reportedly concerned over Taylor Swift’s plans
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have been going pretty strong as they gear up for their big day which is reportedly set to be held in New York City on 3rd July as many are waiting for it. While both of them have amped up their security as their wedding reportedly faces a lot of threats, it seems Travis Kelce has a different view about it.

title: Watch: Trump takes swipe at Indian journalist, sparks racism row
content: During a White House press interaction, President Trump questioned an Indian journalist's nationality before taking her question, later stating he was "kidding." He then described Indian Prime Minister Modi as a "good friend" and expressed confidence in a trade agreement, asserting the US now benefits significantly from its economic ties with India.

title: Bihar's 220-km Riverfront Expressway plan may open next real estate hotspots in the state
content: Bihar is set to transform its infrastructure with three new riverfront expressways spanning 220 km along the Ganga and Gandak rivers. These four-lane corridors, including Vishwamitra Path, Ganga Ambika Path, and Narayani Path, will significantly boost connectivity to Uttar Pradesh and national transport networks, promising a major uplift for the state's real estate sector.

title: Delhi hotel fire:Owner’s past arrest over Indian IDs for Bangladeshis under scanner
content: The owner of a Malviya Nagar hotel where a fire killed 21 people was previously arrested in 2025 for allegedly facilitating the illegal stay of Bangladeshi nationals. Lovkesh Bajaj admitted to knowingly allowing individuals to use his address for fraudulent Indian identity documents in exchange for money. Police are now investigating his role in the deadly hotel fire.

title: BCCI confirms timings of India squad announcement for UK tour
content: The BCCI will announce India's squads for the Ireland and England white-ball tours, after a selection meeting on Saturday. A press conference led by Ajit Agarkar and Devajit Saikia will begin at 1 PM IST. Shreyas Iyer is expected to become T20 captain, while youngsters Tilak Varma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi could earn major roles.

title: More trouble brewing for Mamata's TMC? Expelled MLA Ritabrata makes fresh claim
content: Expelled TMC leader Ritabrata Banerjee expressed confidence that support for his rebel faction will grow, claiming a significant number of MLAs are backing him. He also appealed to CM Suvendu Adhikari to take action against BJP leader Priyanka Tibrewal for protesting outside a fellow rebel's residence.

title: Bulldozer action in Malviya Nagar after Delhi hotel tragedy
content: Amid heavy security, the Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) began sealing unauthorized commercial establishments in South Delhi's Malviya Nagar and surrounding areas. This action follows a deadly fire that exposed widespread violations, prompting the formation of district-level committees to inspect and seal buildings flouting fire safety norms and building bye-laws.

title: ‘Ready to co-develop Su-57 with India, no restrictions’: Putin hardsells 5th-gen fighter jet
content: Russia's President Putin has offered India joint development and production of the fifth-generation Su-57 stealth fighter jet. Despite India's withdrawal from a previous joint project, Russia is open to collaboration, aiming to bolster defence ties amidst India's own AMCA project and regional security concerns.

title: ‘You simply can't’: Ashwin on why forcing Sooryavanshi into Tests may not work
content: Ravichandran Ashwin advocates for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to pursue Test cricket, despite the 15-year-old's phenomenal white-ball success. Sooryavanshi's record-breaking IPL 2026 campaign, where he won the Orange Cap with 776 runs, has sparked debate about his future format. Ashwin acknowledges the changing landscape of cricket and the difficulty in forcing a player's hand.

title: Nushrratt Bharuccha makes confident first public appearance, shows NO signs of being affected
content: Nushrratt Bharuccha made a confident and stylish public appearance at the launch of Ekatra Jewels in Mumbai on June 4, amid a viral audio controversy. The actress, who had clarified that the disputed sounds in her deleted Instagram story were simply those of a puppy, appeared completely unfazed.

title: 'Success of reforms and hard work Indians': PM Modi hails 7.7% GDP growth in FY26
content: India's economy achieved a strong 7.7% growth in FY26. This expansion highlights the nation's inherent economic strength. Prime Minister Narendra Modi attributed this success to effective reforms and the diligent efforts of 140 crore Indians. The government remains committed to improving ease of living and doing business. This growth creates new opportunities for the country's youth.

title: Explained: How Praggnanandhaa can win Norway Chess in a three-way battle
content: Praggnanandhaa remains firmly in contention for the Norway Chess 2026 title heading into the final round. The Indian grandmaster trails leader Wesley So by just half a point, with Alireza Firouzja also in the mix. A classical victory in his final game could propel Praggnanandhaa to the top, though the outcome will also depend on results involving So and Firouzja.

title: Bank loses Rs 87 lakh to SIM swap fraud, HC orders BSNL to pay over Rs 55 lakh compensation
content: Karnataka High Court holds BSNL responsible for an Rs 87 lakh bank fraud, stating the telecom giant's negligence in issuing a duplicate SIM enabled cybercriminals. The court dismissed BSNL's challenge, enhancing compensation to over Rs 50 lakh plus interest, emphasizing a heightened duty of care for financial institutions.

title: 'Preferential access': Goyal says 1st phase of India-US trade deal likely by next month
content: India and the US are making rapid progress towards resolving the remaining issues in their proposed interim trade arrangement, with both countries expected to implement the first phase of the agreement by the middle of next month, Goyal said.

title: Sending ‘kiss emojis’ to another man? J&K HC refuses wife’s plea in divorce dispute
content: The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court has upheld a family court's decision to include a question about a wife allegedly sending "kiss emojis" in a divorce case. The court stated no prejudice would be caused as the husband must prove cruelty. The wife's challenge was dismissed, with the court emphasizing evidence is key.

title: India’s GDP grows at robust 7.8% in Q4 FY26; 7.7% full year growth beats estimates
content: At constant prices, real GDP for the January-March quarter is estimated to have been at Rs 87.77 lakh crore, compared with Rs 81.40 lakh crore in the corresponding period of the previous fiscal year. Nominal GDP during the quarter is estimated at Rs 94.65 lakh crore, reflecting growth of 9.1%.

title: Taylor Swift–Travis Kelce wedding rumours swirl over Blake Lively
content: Reports surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s rumored summer wedding have sparked fresh speculation about Swift’s friendship with Blake Lively. Sources claim Swift wants distance from the ongoing legal battle involving Lively and Justin Baldoni before wedding celebrations begin. Fans also noticed Lively’s absence from reported bachelorette party plans, fueling rumors that the once close friendship may now be strained behind the scenes.

title: India to build 52‑satellite surveillance grid to monitor China, Pakistan threats
content: India is rapidly building a powerful military space presence, launching a 52-satellite constellation from 2025 to 2029, with a significant portion by the private sector. This initiative, guided by a new joint doctrine, aims to enhance surveillance, secure communications, and space awareness, bolstering national security in an increasingly contested orbital domain.

title: ‘Front half of car was under a truck’: Clarke reveals terrifying crash after IPL final
content: Former Australia captain Michael Clarke was involved in a serious road accident in India shortly after the IPL 2026 final. His vehicle crashed into a semi-trailer, leaving him with bruises and the car written off. Clarke expressed gratitude for the support from locals and the BCCI following the frightening incident.

title: Annamalai floats ‘political movement’, to fight next general election in Tamil Nadu
content: The political movement is inspired by the ideals of former President A.P.J. Abdul Kalam. No animosity against the BJP, says Annamalai who is the former president of BJP’s Tamil Nadu unit

title: BJP to plant one million palmyra saplings, protect toddy tappers
content: Madhav says the party will promote palm-based livelihoods and push for Neera production centres to generate employment

title: Between visibility and storytelling: Huma Qureshi and Kritika Kamra on Bollywood’s future
content: Upcoming actors struggle for opportunities because Hindi film production has fallen by half, says Ms. Qureshi; we are making movies for numbers and mid-budget movies have disappeared, says Ms. Kamra

title: Kochi Mayor claims effective pre-monsoon works spared city of major waterlogging
content: Despite the incessant rain, there has been no flooding, unlike in the past when it disrupted traffic, says V.K. Minimol

title: 717 Tasmac liquor shops closed across Tamil Nadu
content: The highest number of shops were closed in the Madurai region with 290, followed by the Coimbatore region with 179

title: Trinamool to move High Court against Bengal Assembly Speaker's recognition of Ritabrata as LoP
content: Reacting to the proposed legal challenge, Ritabrata Banerjee dismissed the allegations and asserted that all constitutional and procedural requirements had been followed

title: CBSE lodges complaint with Delhi police over cyber attacks
content: Large-scale attacks by ‘elements inimical to national interest’; system secure, not compromised: CBSE

title: SC man beaten, hung upside down inside well in Haryana; FIR registered
content: The accused are seen tying the man with ropes, hanging him upside down in a well and assaulting him to extract a confession

title: Patna police book coaching institute owner in firing case; govt. to step in to curb rivalry
content: Khan’s bodyguards had fired in the air after his centre was attacked on June 2; Education Minister says State government will formulate a policy soon to curb rivalry among coaching institutes

title: Environmental protection must go beyond symbolic gestures: Minister O.J. Janeesh
content: State-Level Celebrations of World Environment Day inaugurated in Thrissur; Minister lays foundation for Butterfly Eco Park

title: Three held in connection with fire at private hospital in Muzaffarpur
content: "In pursuance of the FIR that was lodged by the Fire Officer of Muzaffarpur, we arrested Pankaj, the doctor who was in charge of the ICU, in addition to Ram Kumar and Ajit Kumar, the admin manager and the maintenance manager, respectively", says Mr. Ansari

title: Chennai may see 1°C rise in summer temperatures by 2040: report
content: The report, released by Azim Premji University, warns that climate change is no longer a distant threat and India’s coastal regions are already entering a period of significant environmental transition

title: State govt. rejects claims of irregularities in DSC-2025 teacher recruitment
content: Officials say appointments were made strictly on merit and in accordance with Supreme Court guidelines, G.O.Ms. No. 77 and reservation norms; clarify implementation of horizontal reservations, disability quota and sports quota selections

title: In Tripura, Amit Shah announces plans for a ‘smart border’
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said that the government’s ‘smart border’ project is a new security grid which includes cutting-edge tech, local administration and border sentinels

title: Congress leader Praveen Chakravarty files nomination for Rajya Sabha byelection
content: Mr. Chakravarty was accompanied by Chief Minister C. Joseph  Vijay, some Ministers, and former Union Finance Minister P. Chidambaram

title: Two foreign nationals among 795 arrested in Operation Toofan against drug menace in Kerala
content: Home Minister Ramesh Chennithala says the anti-drug enforcement drive has yielded significant results within days of its launch; narcotics substances worth over ₹60 lakh seized within three days

title: Telecom service providers bear civil liability for financial loss in SIM swap frauds caused by their negligence: Karnataka High Court
content: Court directs BSNL to pay ₹55 lakh as compensation to cooperative bank which had lost ₹50 lakh due to SIM swap fraud

title: Controversy over Environment Day event with stage blocking road in Thiruvananthapuram
content: Though programme banner carried the images of Thiruvananthapuram Mayor V.V. Rajesh, who was supposed to inaugurate the event,  Mayor and Corporation distanced themselves from it

title: Free travel for students welcomed while funding issues is flagged
content: The free student bus pass programme was among the key welfare announcements made by the newly sworn-in Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar-led Congress government on Wednesday

title: The Hindu Huddle 2026 Day 1 Highlights: Actors Huma Qureshi, Kritika Kamra discuss the global reach of OTT
content: Talking about pay parity between male and female actors in the film industry, Huma Qureshi said that Films are made through the male gaze. “Women are just accessories. The mindset is deeply rooted”

title: A.P. emerging as gateway on India’s eastern seaboard, will boost India-Russia economic ties: Lokesh
content: Referring to the developments that are shaping the future of global trade, Lokesh highlights avenues for collaboration between the two countries and A.P.'s maritime strengths

title: Kerala’s health sector needs selective private participation, says Muraleedharan
content: With the State’s current financial position, the government will not be able to turn the health sector around single-handedly, says Health Minister. It will be ensured that private participation does not translate into additional health-care expenses for the public, he adds

title: Industry leaders reflect on leadership in times of chaos, disruption and volatility
content: The Hindu Huddle’s session on ‘The Architecture of Leadership: Designing blueprints for a volatile world’

title: Kerala Forest Minister unveils 100-day action plan to tackle rising human-wildlife conflict
content: Department will embark on efforts to assess the carrying capacity of State’s forests to address public concerns regarding animal overpopulation

title: ‘Nilavilakku’ row in Kerala: Samastha clarifies position, says Muslims should avoid religious rituals of other faiths
content: Samastha meeting noted that if a Muslim participates in such a practice while accepting or endorsing the beliefs that are contrary to Islamic teachings, the act could amount to leaving the fold of Islam

title: Palakkad school launches year-long environmental literacy programme
content: Students to assess household carbon footprints as part of United Nations-backed Race to Zero campaign

title: Thunderstorms, rain and heatwave likely to prevail in A.P. for three more days
content: Severe heatwave conditions are likely in 56 mandals on Saturday, while heatwave conditions are expected in 51 mandals, says APSDMA

title: Another regional force crumbling: How TMC is going the Shiv Sena, NCP way
content: the last three years have seen many regional satraps lose power and clout . BJP leader resigned as chief minister paving way for BJP to take up the job for the first time in the state . the BJP's Tejashwi Yadav was literally decimated in the assembly elections .

title: NDA And TIPRA Motha Signal Unity During Amit Shah's Tripura Visit
content: The public display of proximity between the BJP leadership and the TIPRA Motha chief signals they remain committed to the alliance

title: Iran Claims Fired Missiles On 2 US Warships, CENTCOM Says "Did Not"
content: It is the latest episode to shake a ceasefire announced on April 8 that has largely halted hostilities between Iran and the United States.

title: Video: Russia Amusement Park Names Ride After Nuke-Ready Missile 'Oreshnik'
content: The Oreshnik, which Russia first fired against Ukraine in 2024, and most recently last month, has a range of over 5,000 km (3,100 miles).

title: Months On Run, Smuggler Arrested During Ritual To 'Fix Fate' In Ujjain
content: For months, Rajasthan Police chased him like a ghost but every time the police thought they were close, 37-year-old Rakesh Jat vanished.

title: Watch: US Forces Board Oil Tanker Carrying Iranian Crude In Indian Ocean
content: The Davina was sanctioned in 2024 by the US Treasury, which said it had delivered oil from Iran to China.

title: Astronauts On International Space Station In Evacuation Mode As Russians Fix Air Leak
content: The air leaks have been relatively minor in recent months but escalated on Monday from a pound of air per day to two pounds.

title: 18 Lakh At Risk As Nearly 90% Of Delhi Societies Lack Fire Safety Clearances
content: According to data released by the Delhi Fire Services in June 2025, only 101 of the city's 898 functional cooperative group housing societies hold a valid fire NOC.

title: "Help, Help": New Video Shows Desperate Moments Inside Deadly Delhi Hotel Fire
content: New visuals have emerged from the site of the devastating fire that tore through a hotel in South Delhi's congested Hauz Rani area of Malviya Nagar on Wednesday, killing 21 people, including nine Indians and 12 foreign nationals.

title: "With Heavy Heart, Sadness": Another Key Tamil Nadu BJP Leader Quits
content: "This decision has come after careful thought and is not without sadness," Tamil Nadu BJP state secretary Sumathi Venkatesh said

title: Israel Secretly Sent "Elite" Troops To Azerbaijan For Ops In Iran War: Report
content: The quiet deployment in Azerbaijan is only one of the few military positions in the Middle East that gave Israel unprecedented access to Iran. The posting only shows how big of a role the neighbouring countries played to assist in operations against Tehran.

title: 97% Of Women In Punjab To Get Financial Aid From July 1: Bhagwant Mann
content: Under the state government's ambitious scheme, women beneficiaries from the general category will receive a monthly assistance of Rs 1,000, while those from the scheduled castes will get Rs 1,500.

title: India's Semiconductor Sector Eyes $205 Billion Valuation By 2034
content: As of 2025, southern India leads the regional distribution, capturing 36.2 per cent of the semiconductor ecosystem.

title: Only 8 MLAs, 6 MPs At Mamata Banerjee's Home For Key Meet. Party Clarifies
content: The meeting has been called at the Kalighat house of Banerjee. Days before, 60 of the 80 MLAs had skipped the meeting at Banerjee's house.

title: How Ukraine's Satellite Intelligence Move Is Saving Money, Time, Lives
content: The advantages of using this technology and imagery provided by Colorado-based Vantor extend beyond the precision of Ukraine's attacks.

title: Ex-Prince Andrew Sublet Royal Cottages For "Peppercorn Rent": UK Auditors
content: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor lived until recently in the Royal Lodge on the royal family's Windsor estate near London.

title: Punjab Farmers Ready To Pay Thieves Through QR Codes To Save Motors
content: Recently, the situation worsened when approximately 25 motors were stolen again, leaving farmers shocked and helpless.

title: Satellite Data Shows Iran War-Led Slowdown In India's Major Industrial Hubs
content: Remote sensing data analysed through OSINT research by NDTV Datafy shows reduced emissions of nitrogen dioxide (NO2) in at least four industrial clusters.

title: Video: 'Mushroom Cloud' Over Mexico As Tanker Carrying Stolen Fuel Explodes
content: Videos circulating on social media showed the moment the tanker went up in flames near Tepeaca, a small city roughly 35 kilometres east of Puebla.

title: Fuel-saving push, rising costs: Is public transport affordable enough?
content: public transport remains the most economical option on paper . commuters often face additional costs such as last-mile connectivity, multiple transfers and longer travel times . public transport still cheapest mode of urban mobility in India . a commuter travelling 10 to 15 kilometres daily typically spends fraction of what a private vehicle owner spends on fuel alone .

title: 'Carry Tiranga, apply sunscreen': CJP's do's & dont's for June 6 protest; msg for PM Modi
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
